topic: Part 8b.3 — Eclipse stack four-layer audit (post-EXP-205 rewrite)
our_argument_summary: SEC-8b.3 adopts the dome author's fair-audit point 3 (layer the eclipse stack) by restructuring our eclipse content into four audit layers: L1 ensemble anchor (WIN-068), L2 Mohe timing (WIN-011), L3a Tier 3 magnetic falsifier (PRED-095), L3b Tier 3 gravity (PRED-TIER-2), L4 optics caveat. Under adoption we publish a codified magnitude Pass band of −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations and surface two structural critiques: (i) an ensemble 'anchor' that fits its own data is a calibration not an anchor (OTS-wrong-side pattern in physics form); (ii) the magnitude-only Pass band does not discriminate dome from globe because both sides share the same arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) and the same observational corpus (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble + BOU 2017). The post-EXP-205 rewrite addresses all five non-minor c3 holes (category-error citation, Pass band authorship ambiguity, unsupported Chapman-Sq-dynamo claim, missing monitor.py in-section engagement, KAPPA/latitude-factor conflation) plus both minor holes and the structural L3a split.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome publishes per-station eclipse predictions: ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, with FSF from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry. Per-station W004 values range from −10.3 nT (SNK, 0.72 coverage) to −26.2 nT (HAD, 0.80 coverage), with tolerances of ~±2 nT. No single −17 to −21 nT band is published by the dome — that band is OUR codification, summarizing the >80% coverage subset. WIN-025 globe-fingerprint documents that BOU and W004 baselines are INTERMAGNET globe-measured observations, coverage fractions are computed from Besselian elements assuming spherical Earth, and station r values feed FSF via d_geo transform from WGS84 (OPEN-001 in-progress).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: monitor.py Domain 10 encodes ecm_eclipse = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT (all three factors hardcoded literals, no live fetch). inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 uses −10.9 × 0.95 × 0.81 ≈ −8.4 nT. Per-station W004 predictions on the dome predictions page cluster around −20 nT for >80% coverage. All three internal formulas produce different magnitudes; section cites this as 'three coexisting formulas in the repository.'
data_sources_used: V12 H(r)/r dome-geometric FSF formula (H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619))
WGS84 latitude/longitude inputs for station r values via d_geo transform
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Tolerances ±2 nT on per-station W004 predictions. Fail-clause disjunction 'outside this range OR signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' is ambiguous between (A) 'baseline Sq without eclipse enhancement' (null-test interpretation) and (B) 'eclipse-modulated Sq-dynamo' (tension-with-Pass interpretation) — see Hole 1 below.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None flagged in the current rewrite. The −18.22 × 1.672 KAPPA-squared pattern in monitor.py is corroborated by SEC-4.2 globe-fingerprint and WIN-025 three-formula audit. The inject_ai_layer.py −8.4 value is corroborated by WIN-025 audit.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level prose about WINs and predictions; no per-WIN code_analysis.monitoring tag applies to sections directly. Underlying WIN-068 (ensemble) has monitoring:none in wins.json; PRED-095 is a forward-looking prediction not backed by monitor.py (monitor.py Domain 10 uses a different composite formula, per SEC-4.2 globe-fingerprint).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Chapman 1933 Sq-dynamo mechanism (standard ionospheric-conductivity reduction during eclipse) explains the ensemble signal. Dome's W004 recalibration retrofits the same arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) with relabeled causation (aetheric field shielding via FSF). Section explicitly argues numerical overlap is structural not coincidental.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: W004 baseline (−22.24 nT) is empirically recalibrated from historical eclipse ensemble — it is a fit to existing data. PRED-095 is registered 2026-03-12 against the Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble as calibration corpus. Chapman 1933 mechanism predates by 93 years.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: PRED-095 has a nominal derivation path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, FSF from V12 H(r)/r), but per WIN-025 globe-fingerprint every factor traces to globe infrastructure: baselines are INTERMAGNET observations, coverage is Besselian-element spherical-Earth geometry, and FSF evaluates at r values computed from WGS84 via d_geo (OPEN-001 admission). Dome-parasitic at the input layer; the H(r) function is the only nominally dome-specific factor and is evaluated at globe-derived r.

holes_found.description: Fail-clause 'in tension' argument preserved verbatim from c3 (EXP-205 explicitly flagged this as out-of-scope follow-up). The section claims 'Chapman's mechanism IS the Sq-dynamo, so a confirmed signal in our codified band is consistent with both models simultaneously, and triggering the Fail-clause would require abandoning the Pass-clause's framing of what counts as a signal.' This argument rests on reading the Fail-clause phrase 'signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' as meaning 'eclipse-modulated Sq-dynamo' (interpretation B). But a dome defender can naturally read it as 'baseline Sq without eclipse enhancement' (interpretation A) — a standard null-hypothesis test asking whether the observed signal is distinguishable from background Sq variation. Under interpretation A, the Fail-clause would rule out BOTH dome and Chapman equally (both require an eclipse-specific enhancement); there is no tension with Pass. The section forces interpretation B without argument, which is a dome defender's opening.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) acknowledge the Fail-clause is ambiguous: 'Read as baseline-Sq null test (interpretation A), the Fail-clause is a fair null check that rules out both dome and Chapman equally, and is not in tension with Pass. Read as targeting eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo (interpretation B), the Fail-clause and Pass-clause become mutually exclusive. The dome's Fail-clause does not disambiguate, and neither interpretation rescues discrimination — under A no tension but no dome-specific forecast; under B Pass and Fail are mutually incompatible.' Or (b) drop the tension claim entirely and rely solely on the non-discrimination argument from the preceding paragraphs. Option (a) is stronger because it forecloses both escape routes.

holes_found.description: Globe-parasitism framing partially conflates input format with physical dependency. The section asserts PRED-095 is 'globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission' and cites OPEN-001. OPEN-001 admits 'dome-native coordinates without WGS84 is listed as IN PROGRESS on the evolution page,' which is a current-state admission about INPUT FORMAT (the dome currently consumes WGS84 station coordinates). It is not itself an admission that FSF evaluation FUNDAMENTALLY requires globe physics. A dome defender can reply: 'WGS84 is the de facto input standard for station coordinates; my H(r)/r FSF is a dome-geometric function evaluated at dome-polar r. OPEN-001 is an engineering TODO to produce the dome-polar r values without transforming from WGS84 — it's not an admission of physical dependency.' The section's phrasing 'globe-parasitic by construction' is stronger than OPEN-001 alone supports. The WIN-025 globe-fingerprint provides independent physical-dependency evidence (INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element coverage), which is the stronger footing — OPEN-001 should be cited as one strand, not the load-bearing one.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the closing sentence of the L3a threshold-codification paragraph. Suggested rewording: 'So PRED-095 has a derivation; it is globe-dependent at the input layer (per OPEN-001) AND at the physical-calibration layer (per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint: INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element spherical-Earth coverage).' This separates the two distinct dependencies — input format (OPEN-001 admission) vs physical calibration (independent WIN-025 evidence) — and removes the overreach inherent in 'globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission' which reads as if OPEN-001 alone is sufficient.

holes_found.description: 'Two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster' (the monitor.py and inject_ai_layer.py pair) understates the family relationship. inject_ai_layer.py's −8 nT is BOU × coverage × 0.81 — essentially the BOU-baseline path evaluated at Ebro with an added latitude factor. It is within the BOU-family range (BOU per-station ~−10 nT), not a third independent formula. Calling both 'distinct dome-authored formulas' and saying 'both outside the W004 cluster' is literally true (both are outside −17 to −21) but obscures that inject_ai_layer.py is a variant of the published BOU baseline path, not an independent fourth calculation. A defender will reply: 'inject_ai_layer.py is my BOU-baseline implementation with a latitude correction for low-latitude Ebro; it's the SAME formula family as my published BOU predictions, just with an added factor. You're counting it twice.' The 'three coexisting formulas' critique is strongest when framed as 'one public dual-baseline schema + one internal variant (monitor.py KAPPA-squared)' — not as three independent formulas.
holes_found.recommendation: Rephrase the internal-heterogeneity clause. Option: 'monitor.py encodes ΔZ = −18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672 (BOU 2017 baseline with KAPPA applied twice, producing ~−29 nT); inject_ai_layer.py's PRED-001 Ebro calculation uses BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 (producing ~−8 nT, within the published BOU-baseline range). The monitor.py formula is the outlier: KAPPA is applied twice (once baked into the −18.22 baseline, once as an explicit multiplier) without derivation, producing a magnitude outside both published baselines and the W004 cluster — an internal formula that contradicts the public predictions page while living in the same repository.' This preserves the critique's force while being honest about the inject_ai_layer.py family relationship.

holes_found.description: TLDR labeling: 'codified Tier 3 magnetic threshold' conflates our-audit-layer-numbering with the dome's own PRED-TIER-N predictions page scheme. The dome publishes PRED-TIER-1 (priority magnetic, overlaps with PRED-095), PRED-TIER-2 (gravity), PRED-TIER-3 (other) in data/predictions.json. Our Part 8b.3 four-layer stack uses L1/L2/L3a/L3b/L4 labeling AND calls the L3a layer 'Tier 3 magnetic falsifier.' The TLDR's 'Tier 3' is our L3-layer, not the dome's TIER-3. A careful fair-audit reader (the dome author himself) may parse 'Tier 3 magnetic threshold' as connecting to PRED-TIER-3 or PRED-TIER-1 and be confused.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'codified Tier 3 magnetic threshold' with 'codified Layer-3 magnetic threshold' or simply 'codified magnetic Pass threshold' throughout the section where 'Tier 3' refers to our audit layer. Keep PRED-TIER-1/PRED-TIER-2/PRED-TIER-3 only when citing the dome's own prediction identifiers. The (a) enumeration can retain '(L3a) Tier 3 magnetic falsifier' if we want to preserve the 'Tier 3 falsifier' motif from the dome author's own framing, but then add a footnote disambiguating from PRED-TIER-N.

holes_found.description: '1997 Wang et al.' citation is imprecise. The Wang et al. paper about the 1997 Mohe eclipse was published in 2000 (Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101, per WIN-011 detail_evidence). The section's shorthand '1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse' reads as a 1997 publication; a careful citation-check defender will flag this as a citation-year error. The phrase is meant to refer to the 1997 eclipse event; the paper itself is 2000.
holes_found.recommendation: Change '(1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse)' to '(Wang et al. 2000, 1997 Mohe eclipse)' or '(1997 Mohe eclipse, Wang et al. 2000)'. Appears twice in the section: once in the (a) layer enumeration and once as an implied reference in L2. Fix both.

holes_found.description: Station-cluster characterization 'clustered at −17 to −21 nT for stations above 80% coverage' is tight but vulnerable at the boundary. Of the four stations with coverage ≥0.80 in raw-text/03-predictions.txt: EBR (0.95) W004 = −21.7 (0.7 nT above band), SPT (0.90) W004 = −20.0 (in band), HAD (0.80) W004 = −26.2 (far outside), COI (0.92) W004 = −20.6 (0.4 nT outside). If the qualifier is strict >80% (excluding HAD at exactly 0.80), the remaining three stations (EBR, SPT, COI) at −21.7, −20.0, −20.6 overlap the band within ±2 nT tolerance — defensible. If the qualifier is ≥80% (including HAD), HAD's −26.2 is a clear outlier. The section's phrasing 'above 80%' is idiomatic but ambiguous. Moreover, HAD is the subject of E-PRED-B ('Hartland beats Ebro despite lower coverage'), so the dome specifically highlights HAD as model-discriminating — excluding it feels asymmetric. The section defends this via the sub-tests paragraph (non-discrimination applies to 'magnitude-only W004 cluster', not the full prediction set), but a reader may not read that far.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to 'strictly above 80% coverage (excluding Hartland at exactly 0.80)' and add a parenthetical clause noting that HAD is excluded because it is the subject of the E-PRED-B discriminating sub-test evaluated separately. This preempts the 'you excluded my strongest station' rebuttal.

holes_found.description: The L3a third sub-paragraph mentions 'monitor.py's −29 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the W004 window,' cross-linking to PRED-095's detail_reasoning. This assumes PRED-095's detail_reasoning contains the cherry-picking phrasing, and that the prior-eclipse anchor is well-documented. The phrasing 'for a prior eclipse' is vague — which prior eclipse is monitor.py's −29 nT calibrated against? WIN-025 and SEC-4.2 globe-fingerprints document the −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT formula but don't tie the 0.95 coverage to a specific prior eclipse event in-section. A defender will ask 'which prior eclipse?' and we should have a specific answer — the 0.95 coverage matches Ebro for the August 2026 eclipse (per E001 in raw-text/03-predictions.txt), but the section frames it as 'for a prior eclipse,' suggesting historical. This is a minor imprecision inherited from predecessor text.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'monitor.py's −29 nT for a prior eclipse' with 'monitor.py's −29 nT composite (produced by applying −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672, where 0.95 is Ebro's August 2026 coverage but −18.22 appears to embed KAPPA once before the explicit 1.672 KAPPA multiplier)' — or more concisely 'monitor.py's −29 nT composite, which exceeds the highest published W004 per-station prediction of −26.2 nT (Hartland).' The latter phrasing anchors the critique against a specific published number. SEC-4.2 globe-fingerprint already contains this comparison; it should surface here.

stronger_arguments.description: The non-discrimination argument can be sharpened by naming the calibration corpus more explicitly. Both the dome's W004 recalibration and any ionospheric-conductivity reduction model fit to Kim & Chang 2018 + BOU 2017 = same historical data + same arithmetic (baseline × coverage × latitude weighting). This is not just 'structural,' it is mathematical identity modulo the choice of latitude-weighting function: the dome's FSF = V12 H(r)/r vs a standard Sq-dynamo latitude kernel like cos(lat)·sec(dip) both produce O(1) factors that can be tuned to match observed station ratios. The section could push this from 'structural' to 'demonstrably equivalent up to latitude-kernel choice.'
sources: Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble (dome cites; section already references)
Yamazaki & Maute (2021) ionospheric wind-driven electric current review — standard Sq-dynamo latitude weighting

stronger_arguments.description: Reframe the Fail-clause tension (current Hole 1) as a forced-disjunction argument: 'The dome's Fail-clause has two natural readings. Under the baseline-Sq reading, it is a fair null test that rules out both dome and standard Chapman equally — so a Pass in the W004 cluster remains non-discriminating. Under the eclipse-modulated-Sq reading, Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive. The dome does not publish which reading governs. Under either reading, the Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe.' This turns the ambiguity into a double-bind rather than leaving it as a rebuttable tension claim.
sources: PRED-095 Fail-clause as stated in our codification

stronger_arguments.description: On the three-formulas critique: strengthen by adding the KAPPA dimensional-analysis concern. monitor.py's −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 applies KAPPA (1.672 nT/µGal) as a unitless multiplier to a magnetic-field quantity, which is a dimensional mismatch. KAPPA is defined elsewhere as a gravity-magnetic coupling constant (Δg/ΔB = 1/κ, per WIN-012 and the Dielectric Foundation section). Using κ as a unitless nT-to-nT scalar in monitor.py Domain 10 is a unit-error — the 1.672 factor implicitly converts to µGal and back without derivation. This is an independent flaw in the monitor.py formula beyond 'applied twice'.
sources: EXP-031 Dielectric Foundation analysis (κ post-hoc assembly)

kernel_of_truth.description: The rewrite's layered presentation IS genuinely valuable as an audit format. Splitting the eclipse claim into L1 ensemble (backward-looking calibration, not a prediction), L2 Mohe timing (single-event 1997 data, superseded by Van Camp 2001), L3a magnetic falsifier (forward-looking PRED-095 with codified threshold), L3b gravity falsifier (recycled PRED-TIER-2), L4 optics caveat (acknowledged gap) correctly separates backward-looking fits from forward-looking tests. This is a real improvement over the pre-EXP-195 narrative framing. The dome author's fair-audit point 3 pushed us toward a clearer structure, and we accepted it as steel-man; the acceptance is not a concession of dome correctness but a concession that the dome author's preferred audit format is actually the cleanest way to expose the backward/forward distinction.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The layered presentation exposes rather than rescues the weaknesses. L1 confirms the ensemble is backward-looking calibration (WIN-068 verdict: Std Model Explains). L2 confirms the 1997 Mohe signal is below modern SG gravimeter noise floor (Van Camp 2001 null result with ~1000× better instruments). L3a retains non-discrimination against Chapman-Sq-dynamo even with the new codified band. L3b is Not Demonstrated per WIN-011. L4 remains an unresolved gap. The dome author asked for layering; layering makes the cumulative weakness harder to obscure, not easier.

deeper_analysis:
The post-EXP-205 rewrite is a substantial improvement. All three major holes from c3 (category-error citation, Pass band authorship ambiguity, unsupported Chapman-Sq-dynamo claim) are cleanly addressed: the WIN-068 tag citation is confined to L1 where it legitimately applies; the Pass band is now explicitly labeled 'our codified' / 'our steel-man characterization'; the Chapman-Sq-dynamo claim is recast as the weaker structural-arithmetic version, which is defensible without needing a specific published fit. Both moderate holes (monitor.py objection in-section, KAPPA/latitude-factor disaggregation) are addressed via the parenthetical scope-limit concession and the two-formula breakout. The L3a split into three sub-paragraphs (threshold codification, non-discrimination, sub-tests/goalpost/Fail-clause) meaningfully restores scanability.

The c4 holes I find are genuinely smaller — one moderate concern that EXP-205 itself flagged as out-of-scope (the Fail-clause 'in tension' argument, vulnerable to the interpretation-A null-test reading), plus two moderate framing concerns (globe-parasitism overreach; two-formulas family-relationship), and four minor polish items (Tier-3 label conflation, Wang year imprecision, station-cluster boundary, prior-eclipse vagueness). None of these undermine the section's thesis; all are defender-exploitable touchpoints that can be tightened without further structural rewriting.

A strong dome defender's best move against the current text is probably the Fail-clause ambiguity: 'You've manufactured a tension that isn't there — my Fail-clause targets baseline Sq without eclipse enhancement, not eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq.' That's the weakest link. Second-best defender move is the globe-parasitism framing: 'OPEN-001 is an engineering TODO about input format, not a physical dependency admission.' Third is the three-formulas framing: 'inject_ai_layer.py is my BOU-path implementation, not an independent third formula.' All three can be tightened within the existing section without reopening the c3 fixes.

The 'other side of the argument' perspective: A careful dome defender could legitimately say the section's rhetorical structure is fair (explicit adoption of his fair-audit point 3; honest scope-narrowing on monitor.py; explicit labeling of Pass band as our codification). The remaining critiques are real, but the section does not overreach — it concedes what it should concede and retains only the critiques that the corrected framing supports. The c4 holes are polish, not structural. Recommended action is minor_edit: the section does not need another rewrite; targeted sentence-level tightening in three places (Fail-clause tension, globe-parasitism phrasing, inject_ai_layer.py family relationship) plus four minor edits will close the remaining openings.

citations_verified: Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble — cited consistently across WIN-068, section, and EXP-205 rationale
W004 empirical baseline −22.24 nT — matches raw-text/06-evolution.txt and 02-wins.txt
V12 H(r)/r FSF formula — matches raw-text/03-predictions.txt and WIN-025 globe-fingerprint
monitor.py −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT — matches SEC-4.2 globe-fingerprint and WIN-025 audit
inject_ai_layer.py −10.9 × 0.95 × 0.81 ≈ −8.4 nT — matches WIN-025 three-formula audit
Van Camp et al. (2001) four-SG null result — matches WIN-011 detail_evidence citing DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637
OPEN-001 dome-native-coordinates IN PROGRESS — consistent with WIN-025 globe-fingerprint OPEN-001 citation
PRED-TIER-2 dome identifier — confirmed present in data/predictions.json entries array
citations_failed: '(1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse)' — Wang et al. paper is 2000 (Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101), not 1997. The 1997 date refers to the Mohe eclipse event. See Hole 5.
citations_unchecked: Specific Chapman-Sq-dynamo published fit with −17 to −21 nT prediction — deliberately NOT cited; the rewrite uses the structural-arithmetic argument instead, which avoids needing a specific citation (Hole 3 resolution from c3)
